name: FUEL
post_apply_message:
FUEL (2009, Codemasters, non-Steam cracked copy) — Apply auto-adds the Steam shortcut on GE-Proton 10.34. Stage GE first with 'gfm stage-runner'.

THE KEY BIT — THE RIGHT EXE: the shortcut points at SecuLauncher.exe, NOT FUEL.exe. Straight from the crack's own instructions (_HOW TO RUN GAME!!.txt by ChemicalFlood): "To Play The Game, right click and run SecuLauncher.exe as administrator — you cannot launch Fuel.exe as requires Windows Live Launcher." FUEL.exe is still gated behind Games for Windows Live (dead since 2014); SecuLauncher.exe is the crack's way in. If you ever re-add this game by hand, do NOT point it at FUEL.exe — it will not start.

GFWL — THE LAUNCH OPTION + THE RIGHT XLIVELESS: FUEL is a Games for Windows Live game. The launch option WINEDLLOVERRIDES="xlive=n,b" tells Wine to load the game-folder xlive.dll (the GFWL stub) instead of falling through to real GFWL. BUT — tested 2026-07-19, the override alone still hit the 'use the GFWL launcher' prompt. The crack's bundled xlive.dll (340KB) is the wrong build; FUEL needs the specific Proton-proven one (xliveless_20140518_v2, ~172KB, from PCGW's 'Fuel GFWL Fix (XliveLess)').

ONE-TIME SETUP (the account-gated file has to be dropped by hand): download the FUEL GFWL fix — community.pcgamingwiki.com/files/file/2392-fuel-gfwl-fix-xliveless (or the FUEL fandom 'Game Setup' page, file xliveless_20140518_v2). Extract xlive.dll + xlive.ini and drop BOTH into the NAS at 'Game Fixes/fuel/payload/xliveless/'. Edit xlive.ini to set your username (this becomes your save-profile folder name under Documents/My Games/FUEL). Then re-Apply: the recipe's copy_files step overlays them onto the SD copy (backing up the crack's originals as .gfm-bak), leaving the NAS _games/FUEL copy untouched. The step is optional, so Apply won't fail if you haven't dropped the files yet — it just skips.

IF IT WON'T LAUNCH: install everything in the game's _Redist folder (4 programs incl. dotNetFx35 + DirectX) — the crack readme's fix for missing-DLL errors. Under Proton most is already provided by GE, so try without first. There's also a 3.7MB d3d9.dll wrapper in the folder (the *_d3d9.log files are its output); it's left to Proton's DXVK by default — only override d3d9=n,b if rendering is broken.

CONTROLLER + WIDESCREEN: this is a 2009 Codemasters racer — native widescreen and native Xbox 360 pad support, so Steam Input just works. Nothing to mod.

FOV: FUEL's field of view is on the tight side at 16:10; PCGW lists FOV as an area of interest for this game. Nothing bundled — say the word if it bothers you and we'll look.
notes:
Built 2026-07-13 straight off Tony's Windows install at F:\games\FUEL (585 files / 3.84GB), staged to NAS _games/FUEL — no SD scan needed.

LAUNCH TARGET IS THE WHOLE POINT: root exes are FUEL.exe (6.8MB), GameSetup.exe (0.5MB) and SecuLauncher.exe (0.1MB). The crack's readme is explicit that FUEL.exe requires the Windows Live launcher and CANNOT be used; SecuLauncher.exe is the entry point. Razor 1911 release (rzr-fuel.nfo: Codemasters, 2009-07-02, protection SecuROM); 1911.dll at the root is Razor's crack component. Do not 'tidy' the shortcut to FUEL.exe because it looks more canonical — it's the one that doesn't work.

_Redist holds 4 installers for missing-DLL errors per the readme. GFWL is dead (services off 2014) which is exactly why the FUEL.exe path is a dead end even on Windows.

GFWL FIX — ITERATION 2 (2026-07-19): the WINEDLLOVERRIDES="xlive=n,b" launch option was verified APPLIED (log: Steam closed, shortcut written with the option, restarted) but FUEL STILL hit the 'use the GFWL launcher' prompt. So the override is necessary but not sufficient — the crack's bundled xlive.dll (340KB) is the wrong build. Community consensus (PCGW 'Fuel GFWL Fix (XliveLess)' file 2392, FUEL Deck guide 3007886375, Lucis Caelum's save guide 268031297): use xliveless_20140518_v2 (~172KB) specifically. Good news from the symptom: 'GFWL' not 'SecuROM' means the Razor SecuLauncher crack IS working — only GFWL remains.

Added an OPTIONAL copy_files step (from payload/xliveless -> {game_dir}, backup_originals) that overlays the correct xlive.dll+ini onto the SD copy. The file is ACCOUNT-GATED on PCGW (csrf dance returns a login page — can't auto-fetch), so it's a BYO local-payload: Tony drops xlive.dll+xlive.ini into NAS Game Fixes/fuel/payload/xliveless/ (off-git, copyright-adjacent GFWL emulator, same handling as other cracks). copy_files resolves local-payloads first, so that's where it reads. NAS _games/FUEL game copy stays clean (Tony's rule) — the fix files live in the recipe payload area and land on the SD only. UNTESTED whether the v2 xliveless actually fixes it under Proton; if not, next steps are protontricks-installing it into the prefix, or a modified FUEL.exe (per the Deck guide comments). FUEL is a hard GFWL+SecuROM case — low ROI, park if it keeps fighting. Non-Steam -> computed appid (not in the gospel registry). Native widescreen + 360 pad (2009 Codemasters racer) so no controller/widescreen work needed. Copy speed note: 585 files/3.84GB staged at ~95 MB/s.
